answer:
**The work is *Vrhaspatitattva*, Chapter 1.**[^1]

In this passage, the divine instructor Śrīkaṇṭha speaks in the first person to his pupil Vṛhaspati, identifying himself as the seventh in an eight-fold succession of teaching deities: **"Ananta, Sūkṣma, Śivatama, Ekarudra, Ekanetra, Trimūrti, Śrīkaṇṭha, and Śikhaṇḍī"**. He confesses: **"But I was shot with passion by Lord Kāma. I gazed upon the holy Kāma, and because he was gazed upon with my poison-sight, the body of the holy Kāmadeva was shattered, incinerated, and turned to ash. But the passion (*rāga*) remained in me. That is why I took your mother, the Lady Umā, as my wife, and we had a son, the holy Sanatkumāra."**[^1]

[^1]: *Vrhaspatitattva*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-4d2295).

args.query: "god of love" "turned to ash" OR "incinerated" Kāmadeva
"seventh" OR "seven" Ananta OR Sukṣma OR Sivatama OR Ekarudra OR Ekanetra OR Trimūrti OR Srikantha OR Sikhandi
